solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String smallestSubsequence(String s) {
        boolean[] inResult = new boolean[26];
        int[] count = new int[26];
        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
            count[c - 'a']++;
        }
        java.util.Stack<Character> stack = new java.util.Stack<>();
        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
            int idx = c - 'a';
            if (inResult[idx]) {
                count[idx]--;
                continue;
            }
            while (!stack.isEmpty() && stack.peek() > c && count[stack.peek() - 'a'] > 0) {
                char top = stack.pop();
                inResult[top - 'a'] = false;
            }
            stack.push(c);
            inResult[idx] = true;
            count[idx]--;
        }
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        for (char c : stack) {
            sb.append(c);
        }
        return sb.toString();
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a greedy approach with a stack to maintain the lexicographically smallest subsequence. It ensures each character is added only once and maintains counts to allow removal of larger characters when possible. The algorithm is efficient with linear time complexity and constant space.>
